frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
A: These girls are putting so much makeup nowadays, ma. From far one face, after washing completely different only.
B: True, akka.
A: Little powder, kajal is enough to look neat. If a girl is naturally nice, why all this over? But at the same time she should maintain herself also, otherwise people will say she has no care for her appearance.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it subjects women to aesthetic policing and a double bind: too much makeup is framed as deceptive, while too little grooming is criticized as neglect. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference and appearance obligation surface manifestations.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter Meena who is 20 is dark skinned like her father. Meena has started applying Fair and Lovely on her face every morning. You noticed two weeks ago and have not said anything. Today you bought her a larger tube without her asking. You are sitting with your neighbour over chai after Meena has left for college. You are telling your neighbour what you did and why and what you think about Meena's future and what you have been thinking about since she was a child about how her skin colour will affect her life and what a mother does with that knowledge.
